title: The Adventures of Ferdinand Count Fathom — Complete
author: Smollett, T. (Tobias)
summary: Tobias Smollett's The Adventures of Ferdinand Count Fathom follows a cunning, unscrupulous protagonist whose cold-blooded roguery propels a rambling series of schemes across Europe. Reared with a fierce camp-follower mother, Fathom practices seduction, imposture, theft, and military stratagems in Vienna, Paris, and England. He entangles lovers, confederates, nobles, and a virtuous friend Renaldo (Count de Melvil) in plots involving assignations, smuggling, and legal intrigues. Persistent spite and blind ambition bring temporary triumphs but eventual ruin: fortunes strangled, a matrimonial snare, and moral exposure. The novel ends with recognitions, retrospective revelations, and fitting consequences for vice and virtue and poetic justice.

characters.name: Ferdinand Count Fathom
characters.personality: cunning, unscrupulous adventurer; cold-blooded, ambitious, skilled in imposture and seduction
characters.lifespan: rises by fraud, seduction, and stratagems across Europe; ultimately suffers financial and moral ruin (fortunes 'strangled', trapped by a matrimonial noose) and is 'safely housed' as narrative concludes

characters.name: Fathom's Mother
characters.personality: hardened camp-follower; Amazon-like, rapacious and bold
characters.lifespan: described as killed by an officer of the hussars (death on the battlefield)

characters.name: Renaldo (Count de Melvil)
characters.personality: virtuous, noble friend and foil to Fathom; steadfast and honourable
characters.lifespan: suffers distress and deception, reduced nearly to despair by events and a letter, but survives, returns to England, uncovers mysteries, and achieves recognition

characters.name: Monimia
characters.personality: virtuous heroine, the object of protective and romantic interest
characters.lifespan: her honour is protected by providence; later she is represented as dead (a midnight pilgrimage to Monimia's tomb)

characters.name: Mademoiselle de Melvil
characters.personality: less vivified sister-in-law; composed and conventional
characters.lifespan: present in the narrative as relation of Renaldo/Count de Melvil; specific fate not detailed in provided text

characters.name: Joshua
characters.lifespan: portrayed as virtuous; exact fate not specified in provided excerpt

characters.name: Don Diego
characters.personality: tedious, foolish, comic or pitiable figure
characters.lifespan: his history is treated as essential to the plot; precise fate not given in excerpt

characters.name: Elenor
characters.personality: fair and virtuous; a victim of Fathom's assaulting designs
characters.lifespan: subject of a singular attack and triumph by Fathom; subsequent fate not fully detailed in excerpt

characters.name: Noble Castilian
characters.personality: remarkable personage with a related history
characters.lifespan: his history is recounted (Chapter 26); fate not specified in provided text

characters.name: The Jeweller
characters.personality: rich tradesman whose household is infiltrated by Fathom
characters.lifespan: house is the scene of Fathom's intrigue (daughter and wife endangered); specific outcome not detailed in excerpt

characters.name: Daughter of the Jeweller
characters.lifespan: exposed to a perilous incident during Fathom's intrigue; exact fate unclear in excerpt

characters.name: Wife of the Jeweller
characters.personality: another object of Fathom's assignation and plot
characters.lifespan: entangled by Fathom; fate unspecified in provided excerpt

characters.name: Female Associate / Confederate
characters.personality: partner in Fathom's schemes and consort in crime
characters.lifespan: participates in several plot attempts; individual fate(s) not specified in excerpt

characters.name: Step-dame
characters.lifespan: sets a snare for Fathom but is thwarted by his 'Good Genius'; further fate not detailed

characters.name: Smuggler
characters.personality: ingenious conjecturer who almost detects Fathom
characters.lifespan: nearly exposes Fathom's schemes; a providential deliverance prevents full capture as described

characters.name: Fathom's Good Genius
characters.personality: benevolent, providential force/personification aiding Fathom at times
characters.lifespan: interposes to save the hero from traps on occasion (as narrated)

characters.name: Matthew Bramble, Esq.
characters.personality: referenced (from Humphry Clinker) as comparative testimony
characters.lifespan: not a character in this novel; mentioned in the introduction as a literary comparison

characters.name: Doctor ——— (dedicatory addressee)
characters.personality: addressee of the dedication; representative figure
characters.lifespan: appears only as the dedicatee in the book's front matter
